question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the address_book_entry table has a node_account_id of NULL and the node table also has no account_id for that node, causing COALESCE(n.account_id, abe.node_account_id) to return NULL and the exported nodeAccountId to be NULL, which may break consumers expecting a valid account ID?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause incorrect records to be exported by querying when the address_book_service_endpoint table has rows with the same ip_address_v4 and port for a given node, causing the jsonb_agg with ORDER BY to include duplicate endpoint entries in the serviceEndpointsJson field of the exported record?
